---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r libraries}
# ==============================================================================
# PART 1: UNIVERSAL DATA PREP (Weekly + EEZ Buffer)
# ==============================================================================

rm(list = ls())

# CONFIGURATION
# Set to FALSE to process ALL birds (Takes hours). 
# Set to TRUE to process only 5 birds (for testing).
TEST_MODE <- FALSE

# Libraries
library(dplyr)
library(purrr)
library(readr)
library(sf)
library(terra)
library(rebird)
library(ebirdst)
library(rnaturalearth)

# Ensure Key is set (Uncomment if needed)
Sys.setenv(EBIRD_KEY = "ar8ei8j24plp")
```

```{r spatial setup}
# ==============================================================================
# 1. SPATIAL SETUP (Land + EEZ Buffer)
# ==============================================================================
message("Defining study area with coastal buffer...")

# Get US Lower 48 Land
us_lower48_land <- ne_states(country = "United States of America", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(!postal %in% c("AK", "HI")) %>%
  st_transform(3857) # Web Mercator for accurate buffering in meters

# Create 200 Nautical Mile Buffer (approx 370,400 meters) for Pelagics
# We union the states first to get one big shape, then buffer it
us_buffer <- st_union(us_lower48_land) %>%
  st_buffer(dist = 370400) 

# The final study mask is the Land + The Buffer
study_area_mask <- st_union(us_buffer)

# FIX: Convert sf object to terra SpatVector explicitly
# This prevents the "unable to find inherited method" error in the loop
study_area_vect <- vect(study_area_mask)
```

```{r species list generation}
# ==============================================================================
# 2. SPECIES LIST GENERATION (FROM AVIBASE CSV)
# ==============================================================================
message("Processing species list from avibase.csv...")

# 1. Load the target list (Avibase export)
if (!file.exists("avibase.csv")) {
  stop("avibase.csv file not found. Please ensure your target list is in the working directory.")
}

avibase_targets <- read_csv("avibase_us48.csv", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  select(sciName, comName) %>%
  distinct()

# 2. Load eBird taxonomy to map scientific names to unique 'speciesCode'
# Note: ebirdtaxonomy() pulls the full taxonomy from the rebird package
ebird_taxonomy <- ebirdtaxonomy() %>%
  as_tibble() %>%
  filter(category == "species") %>%
  select(speciesCode, comName, sciName)

# 3. Join Targets with Taxonomy
# This adds the crucial 'speciesCode' to your target list
targets_with_codes <- avibase_targets %>%
  inner_join(ebird_taxonomy, by = c("sciName", "comName"))

# 4. Filter for availability in Status & Trends (S&T)
# We can only map species that have modeled abundance data
species_with_st_data <- targets_with_codes %>%
  semi_join(ebirdst_runs, by = c("speciesCode" = "species_code"))

# Create the final list of codes to process
target_species_list <- species_with_st_data$speciesCode

message(paste("Found", length(target_species_list), "species in your list with S&T data."))

# Apply Test Mode Limiter
if (TEST_MODE) {
  message("!!! RUNNING IN TEST MODE (First 20 Species Only) !!!")
  target_species_list <- head(target_species_list, 300)
}
```

```{r template creation}
# ==============================================================================
# 3. TEMPLATE CREATION (9km)
# ==============================================================================
message("Setting up 9km grid template...")

if(length(target_species_list) == 0) {
  stop("No valid species found! Check your CSV or S&T data availability.")
}

# Use the first bird to define the grid
ref_species <- target_species_list[1]

tryCatch({
  load_raster(ref_species, product = "abundance", resolution = "9km")
}, error = function(e) {
  message(paste("Downloading reference species:", ref_species))
  ebirdst_download_status(ref_species, pattern = "abundance_median_9km")
})

ref_rast <- load_raster(ref_species, product = "abundance", resolution = "9km")

# Project Study Area to match Native Raster (Sinusoidal) for cropping
boundary_native <- st_transform(study_area_mask, st_crs(ref_rast))

# Create Native Template
template_native <- crop(ref_rast[[1]], boundary_native)
values(template_native) <- NA

# Project to Web Mercator (EPSG:3857) for the App
template_webmerc <- project(template_native, "EPSG:3857", method = "near")
values(template_webmerc) <- 1:ncell(template_webmerc)
names(template_webmerc) <- "cell_id"
```

```{r processing loop}
# ==============================================================================
# 4. PROCESSING LOOP (Weekly Data)
# ==============================================================================
species_occurrence_list <- list()
dir.create("app_data", showWarnings = FALSE)

counter <- 0
total <- length(target_species_list)

message("Starting processing loop. This will take time.")

# SAFETY CHECK: Ensure study_area_vect exists if running chunks out of order
if (!exists("study_area_vect")) {
  if (exists("study_area_mask")) {
    message("Re-creating missing study_area_vect from study_area_mask...")
    study_area_vect <- vect(study_area_mask)
  } else {
    stop("Error: 'study_area_vect' is missing. Please re-run Section 1 (Spatial Setup) to define the boundary.")
  }
}

for (code in target_species_list) {
  counter <- counter + 1
  
  # --- A. Download ---
  check_load <- try(load_raster(code, product = "abundance", resolution = "9km"), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(check_load, "try-error")) {
    message(sprintf("[%d/%d] Downloading %s...", counter, total, code))
    dl_status <- try(ebirdst_download_status(code, pattern = "abundance_median_9km"), silent = TRUE)
    if (inherits(dl_status, "try-error")) next 
  } else {
    if(counter %% 10 == 0) message(sprintf("[%d/%d] Processing %s...", counter, total, code))
  }
  
  # --- B. Load & Project ---
  r <- try(load_raster(code, product = "abundance", resolution = "9km"), silent = TRUE)
  if (inherits(r, "try-error")) next
  
  # Project to Web Mercator
  r_proj <- project(r, template_webmerc, method = "near")
  
  # FIX: Mask using the SpatVector (study_area_vect) instead of the sf object
  # Keeps land + 200mi ocean, removes distant noise
  r_mask <- mask(r_proj, study_area_vect)
  
  # --- C. Extract Weekly Data ---
  # S&T Abundance usually has 52 layers (Weeks)
  # If 52 layers verify, otherwise skip
  if (nlyr(r_mask) != 52) {
    # Some older models might be monthly? Skip for safety or adjust logic.
    next 
  }
  
  # Collect presence indices for this bird
  bird_weeks <- list()
  
  for (w_idx in 1:52) {
    v <- values(r_mask[[w_idx]])
    
    # Threshold: Abundance > 0
    present_indices <- which(v > 0)
    
    if (length(present_indices) > 0) {
      cell_ids <- values(template_webmerc)[present_indices]
      
      # Store temporarily
      bird_weeks[[length(bird_weeks) + 1]] <- data.frame(
        cell_id = cell_ids,
        week = w_idx
      )
    }
  }
  
  # If bird is present anywhere, add to master list
  if (length(bird_weeks) > 0) {
    df_bird <- dplyr::bind_rows(bird_weeks)
    df_bird$species_code <- code
    species_occurrence_list[[length(species_occurrence_list) + 1]] <- df_bird
  }
  
  # Garbage collect to keep RAM happy
  rm(r, r_proj, r_mask)
  gc()
}
```

```{r save outputs}
# ==============================================================================
# 5. SAVE OUTPUTS
# ==============================================================================
message("Binding final database...")
master_lookup <- bind_rows(species_occurrence_list)

message("Saving app_data/master_lookup.rds (Compressed)...")
saveRDS(master_lookup, "app_data/master_lookup.rds")

message("Saving app_data/grid_template.tif...")
# EXPLICIT DATATYPE: Ensures the grid is saved as integers (lighter, cleaner matching)
writeRaster(template_webmerc, "app_data/grid_template.tif", overwrite=TRUE, 
            gdal=c("COMPRESS=DEFLATE"), datatype="INT4U")

# Metadata
processed_codes <- unique(master_lookup$species_code)
ebird_taxonomy <- ebirdtaxonomy() 
species_meta <- ebird_taxonomy %>% 
  filter(speciesCode %in% processed_codes) %>%
  select(speciesCode, comName, sciName)

saveRDS(species_meta, "app_data/species_names.rds")

# Placeholder Life List (Empty) - Careful not to overwrite if you have one you like
if(!file.exists("app_data/my_life_list.rds")) {
  saveRDS(character(0), "app_data/my_life_list.rds")
}

message("Processing Complete. You can now run app.R")
```
